facts.text: North Miami was originally promoted as the town of Detroit before adopting its current name.
The community’s roots trace back to the historic Arch Creek area, a foundational part of its early settlement.
Caribbean and Latin American cultures shape North Miami’s food, businesses, and neighborhood character, making it one of South Florida’s most culturally rich communities.
During its centennial, the city hosted Historical City Tours on City Trolleys, letting residents and visitors explore its past.
Florida International University’s Biscayne Bay Campus, the North Miami Public Library, and MOCA are key institutions that support the city’s education and cultural life.
North Miami’s compact area packs a broad mix of restaurants, languages, and neighborhood character, all within a few miles of each other.
